title: 4-Sulpho-2-Aminobenzenethiol-A New Reagent for Gravimetric Determination of Mercury(II)
author: A. K. CHAKRABARTI
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Jadavpur University, Calcutta-700 032 

  Manuscript received 15 April 1981, revised 28 August 1981, accepted 6 November 1981  

 4-Sulpho-2-aminobenzenethiol as a ligand has been used in the gravimetric determination of 6-30 mg Hg(II). The greenish yellow precipitate, Hg(C 6 H 6 O 3 NS 2 ) 2   is quantitatively precipitated in the pH range 2.0-6.0, adjusted with aqueous ammonia, and is weighed as such after drying at 105-110˚. Interferences by Zn 2+ , Cd 2+ , As 2+ , Sb 2+ ,Bi 2+ , Cu 2+ , Ni 2+ , Co 2+ , Mn 2+ , Pb 2+ , Tl + , Pd 2+ , Fe 3+  Al 3+ , Cr 3+ , UO \(^{2+}_2\) ,  MoO \(^{3-}_4\)   -, WO \(^{2-}_4\) . Be 2+  and Mg 2+  could be overcome by using suitable masking agent. F - , PO  \(^{3-}_4\) . ASO \(^{3-}_4\) , tartarate, citrate and oxalate do not interfere. The standard deviation is  \(\pm\) 0.30%.
